name: Coll de Nargó - Collado de Bòixols - Montanissell - Coll de Nargó — Ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Coll De Nargó are set within the diverse and challenging terrain of the Catalan Pre-Pyrenees. The region features mountainous landscapes with significant elevation gains, including passes like Coll de Bòixols. Riders encounter varied surfaces such as rock, roots, mud, and loose stones across its trails. Natural features like the Riu Sallent-la tolla-riu Valldarques and the Gorgs of Coll de Nargó are also present. Best mountain bike trails around Coll De Nargó The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Boumort — Ruta circular desde Organyà, a 42.6 miles (68.5 km) trail that takes 7 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 2,100 meters of elevation gain through the Sierra de Boumort. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Coll de Nargó - Collado de Bòixols - Montanissell - Coll de Nargó — Ruta circular, a difficult 25.8 miles (41.6 km) path. This route offers panoramic views as it climbs to Collado de Bòixols. Local mountain bikers also love the Lo Coscollet – Tossal del Faig loop from Coll de Nargó, a 24.6 miles (39.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Coll De Nargó is defined by its Pre-Pyrenean setting, mountainous terrain, and diverse ecosystems. The network offers options for moderate to hard routes, catering to different skill levels. The routes in Coll De Nargó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 190 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Coll De Nargó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Coll de Nargó?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike routes available around Coll de Nargó. These trails cater to various skill levels, with a majority being difficult, and several moderate options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Coll de Nargó?
A: The terrain in Coll de Nargó is diverse and challenging, characteristic of the Catalan Pre-Pyrenees. You can expect mountainous landscapes with significant elevation gains, varied surfaces including rock, roots, mud, and loose stones. Routes often feature fast and flowing sections, as well as tight and technical parts.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Coll de Nargó?
A: The most popular months for mountain biking in Coll de Nargó are June and August, offering generally favorable weather conditions for exploring the trails. However, the region's diverse ecosystems mean it can be enjoyed across many seasons, with considerations for snow at higher elevations in winter.

Q: Are there any natural features or landmarks to see while mountain biking in Coll de Nargó?
A: Yes, the area is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguja del Corb and Ruins of Ermita de Sant Salvador&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/153045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocas de Sant Honorat&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural water bodies like the Riu Sallent-la tolla-riu Valldarques and the Gorgs (pools) of Coll de Nargó.

Q: Can I find waterfalls along the mountain bike routes in Coll de Nargó?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4689400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfalls of Organyà&lt;/a&gt; are a notable natural feature in the vicinity of Coll de Nargó, offering a refreshing stop or scenic detour.

Q: Are there any good viewpoints on the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain of Coll de Nargó offers numerous panoramic viewpoints. Some routes climb to passes like Collado de Bòixols, providing expansive views. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3037609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Top of the Coscollet viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/153045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Rocas de Sant Honorat&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de las Masias loop from Organyà&lt;/a&gt; cover over 40 km with significant elevation. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de la Creu de Ferri – Coll de Paller loop from Organyà&lt;/a&gt;, which is also over 40 km and features substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Coll de Nargó?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950358108/sierra-de-boumort-ruta-circular-desde-organya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Boumort — Ruta circular desde Organyà&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949564190/coll-de-nargo-collado-de-boixols-montanissell-coll-de-nargo-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Nargó - Collado de Bòixols - Montanissell - Coll de Nargó — Ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Coll de Nargó?
A: The mountain bike trails around Coll de Nargó are predominantly rated as moderate to difficult, often involving significant elevation gains and technical sections. While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the immediate area, families with older, experienced riders might find some of the moderate trails suitable, depending on their skill level and fitness.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Coll de Nargó?
A: While many outdoor areas in the Catalan Pre-Pyrenees are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. Ensure your dog is well-behaved, kept on a leash where required, and that you carry out all waste to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What facilities are available near the mountain bike trails in Coll de Nargó?
A: Coll de Nargó, as a small town, offers basic amenities. You can find local cafes and restaurants for refreshments and meals. For accommodation, there are guesthouses or rural lodgings in and around the town. It's advisable to check availability, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Coll de Nargó?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in Coll de Nargó, particularly in the town center or designated areas. For specific trailheads, it's often possible to find roadside parking or small lots, but these can vary. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct local access.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Coll de Nargó?
A: The mountain bike trails around Coll de Nargó are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging terrain, the stunning Pre-Pyrenean landscapes, and the variety of routes that cater to different levels of experienced mountain bikers.
tips: Stone bridge. With the name, Puente de las Masias, it crosses the homonymous ravine, which carries water from the Valldarques River. Within the municipality of Coll de Nargó.

Ideal place for cyclists. We can enjoy an incredible landscape and views throughout the climb to the pass. Located at a height of 1,321 metres, in the Catalan pre-Pyrenees, it joins the counties of Pallars Jussà and Alt Urgell.

In the middle of a beautiful natural setting, at the foot of the Sierra de Sant Joan, we find this beautiful church, in Romanesque style and from the 12th century.
